name: nanonis-spm

metadata.name: nanonis-spm
metadata.summary: Python package for controlling Nanonis SPM software through Python

documentation:
# Python Interface Package for Nanonis 

Official python package for the Nanonis SPM Controller software.

## Usage

This package allows users of the Nanonis SPM Controller software to use and control
said software through python commands.

## How to use

### Importing

import nanonis_spm

### Initializing Connection through the socket module

import socket

connection = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)

connection.connect((IP_ADRESS_HERE, PORT_HERE))

nanonisInstance = nanonis_spm.Nanonis(connection)

NOTE : THE PORT HAS TO BE AN INTEGER AND THE IP ADRESS A STRING

### Enabling Debug Console Output

The function "returnDebugInfo()" takes an integer as an argument. 
This integer should be either 1 = on, or 0 = off. This option is off by default.

Enable by running:
nanonisInstance.returnDebugInfo(1)

### Examples

There is a collection of examples installed with the package.

The description of all the available functions can be found in the TCP Protocol Document, and hovering on the function depending on the used IDE.

IMPORTANT:
The TCP Interface requires every argument to be of certain size (see documentation).
This is why the Numpy dependency is required, since it enables the specification
of variable sizes. 

Example:

nanonisInstance.BiasSwp_LimitsSet(np.float32(1), np.float32(2))

We hereby ensure that the arguments are of the correct size.

## Change Log

### 1.0.3
Added the function Util.VersionGet to get the software version, the MCVA5 preamplifier functions, the V5e Generic PI Controller functions, and all the MultiProbe functions (Bias, Current, Z-Controller, and scanner control) needed in a MultiProbe system.

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_AutoApproach
documentation: Automatic approach implemented using Nanonis Programming Interface functions.
This can be used if you want to execute special functions after each
motor step or if you want to control your motor remotely.

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_AutoApproach.Motor_NrSteps
documentation: number of steps to move toward the sample

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipShaper
documentation: This procedure is mostly used to shape a STM tip,
dipping it into the sample and pulling it back slowly
First ramp Z (usually to approach the tip), then apply a bias voltage, and
finally ramp Z again (usually to withdraw the tip)

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipShaper.BeforeRamp_ZCrl_SwitchOffDelay
documentation: seconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipShaper.Ramp1_TipLift
documentation: meters

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipShaper.Ramp1_LiftTime
documentation: seconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipShaper.Bias_SettlingTime
documentation: seconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipShaper.Ramp2_TipLift
documentation: meters

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipShaper.Ramp2_LiftTime
documentation: seconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipShaper.AfterRamp_WaitTime
documentation: seconds

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_GenSweeper
documentation: This example performs a 2D sweep using a User output and the Generic Sweeper.
For each value of the User output (y axis)
it will perform a Generic Sweep (x axis).

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_GenSweeper.Sweep_X_InitSettling
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_GenSweeper.Sweep_X_Settling
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_GenSweeper.Sweep_X_Period
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_GenSweeper.Sweep_X_MaxSlewRate
documentation: units/second

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_Custom2DSweep_GenSweeper.GenSweeper_NrAcqChannels
documentation: the swept channel is part of the acquired data

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ZDriftMeasurement
documentation: This example records the Z channel (i.e. the tip height) and
calculates the drift along this axis.
The drift speed can then be applied to compensate for it.
Before starting the measurement, the tip must be in contact with the sample
and the Z-Controller must be running.

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ZDriftMeasurement.Z_Drift_Duration
documentation: the time the Z drift measurement is running (in minutes)

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ZDriftMeasurement.Z_Drift_IterationTime
documentation: how often we read the Z position to calculate drift (in seconds)

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ZDriftMeasurement.Z_Drift_Apply
documentation: if True, the Z drift is applied after the Z drift measurement

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ZDriftMeasurement.Z_Drift_Value
documentation: meters/second

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ZDriftMeasurement.Z_Drift_Comp_OnOff
documentation: Drift compensation status (on or off)

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ZDriftMeasurement.Z_Drift_Comp_Vz
documentation: Current linear speed applied to the Z piezo

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasSpectroscopy_MultiSegment
documentation: This example performs a bias spectroscopy consisting of several user-definable segments
using the Bias Spectroscopy module.

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasSpectroscopy_MultiSegment.Spectr_Channels
documentation: Acquired channels: Current and Bias

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasSpectroscopy_MultiSegment.ZCtrl_TipLift
documentation: meters

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasSpectroscopy_MultiSegment.ZCtrl_SwitchOffDelay
documentation: seconds

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_CustomZSweep
documentation: This example performs a custom Z sweep.
The selected channels are acquired at each Z sweep point.

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_CustomZSweep.Sweep_SettlingTime
documentation: seconds to wait after sweeping to a point

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_CustomZSweep.Sweep_AcqSignals
documentation: signals to acquire, Current & Z

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_SwitchMode_STM_AFM
documentation: This example switches between STM mode and AFM mode

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_SwitchMode_STM_AFM.Switch_To_AFM
documentation: to AFM=1, to STM=0

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_SwitchMode_STM_AFM.ZController_TipLift
documentation: meters

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_LockInSweep
documentation: This example performs a sweep of the selected Lock-In (LI) Frequency Generator.
It creates an array of frequency values within a certain range, it waits Settling Time,
and it acquires and averages the selected channels.

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_LockInSweep.Sweep_LI_Index
documentation: index of LI Freq. Gen. to sweep

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_LockInSweep.Sweep_InitSettlingTime
documentation: seconds to wait before starting the sweep

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_LockInSweep.Averaging
documentation: number of samples to average

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_LockInSweep.Sweep_SettlingTime
documentation: seconds to wait after sweeping to each point

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_LockInSweep.Sweep_AcqSignals
documentation: signals to acquire, LI Demod. 1 X&Y

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasPulsesAtVariousZ
documentation: This example applies bias voltage pulses at various Z positions

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasPulsesAtVariousZ.Bias_PulseWidth
documentation: seconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasPulsesAtVariousZ.Z_Incr
documentation: meters

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasPulsesAtVariousZ.Z_SettlingTime
documentation: seconds to wait after setting Z position

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipCleaner
documentation: Moves the tip to a specified location and performs Bias pulses there
Useful to try to clean the tip
without affecting the sample at the current position

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipCleaner.XY_GoToPos
documentation: meters

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipCleaner.BiasPulse_Width
documentation: seconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipCleaner.BiasPulse_ZCtrl
documentation: hold the Z-Ctrl

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_TipCleaner.BiasPulse_Pause
documentation: seconds to wait between pulses

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep
documentation: This example performs a Frequency Sweep using the built-in functionality
in the RF Generator module

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep.Limit_Lower
documentation: Hz

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep.Limit_Higher
documentation: Hz

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep.Mode
documentation: Linear

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep.Dwell
documentation: s

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep.Infinite
documentation: Off

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep.OffTime
documentation: s

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep.AutoOffTime
documentation: On

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep.Direction
documentation: Sweep Up

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_GenSweeper
documentation: This example performs multiple Power Sweeps by using the Generic Sweeper
to sweep the power in the RF Generator module multiple times with different limits

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_GenSweeper.Sweep_Start
documentation: Sweep start values for the different sweeps to perfom

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_GenSweeper.Sweep_Stop
documentation: (the list size determines the number of sweeps)

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_GenSweeper.Sweep_InitSettling
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_GenSweeper.Sweep_Settling
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_GenSweeper.Sweep_Period
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_GenSweeper.Sweep_MaxSlewRate
documentation: units/second

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_GenSweeper.GenSweeper_NrAcqChannels
documentation: the swept channel is part of the acquired data

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_CustomBiasSweep
documentation: This example performs a custom bias sweep. You can create an array of
Bias voltages and it will record the selected channels at each Bias voltage.

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_CustomBiasSweep.Sweep_SettlingTime
documentation: seconds to wait after sweeping to a point

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_CustomBiasSweep.Sweep_AcqSignals
documentation: signals to acquire, Current & Bias

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_SimpleSignalAcquisition
documentation: This example acquires one channel (i.e. the tip height) with
a defined oversampling and logs the oversample value
in a variable for as many samples as defined or until
manually stopped (pressing the Spacebar)

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_SimpleSignalAcquisition.Channel_Index
documentation: tip height

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasSpectroscopy
documentation: This example performs a bias spectroscopy using the Bias Spectroscopy module.
It configures and runs Bias Spectroscopy and then saves the acquired data into a file by using its auto-save functionality.

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasSpectroscopy.Spectr_Channels
documentation: Acquired channels: Current and Bias

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasSpectroscopy.ZCtrl_TipLift
documentation: meters

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_BiasSpectroscopy.ZCtrl_SwitchOffDelay
documentation: seconds

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ForceDistanceCurve_OffsetComp
documentation: This example perfoms force-distance measurement with offset compensation.
It requires the PLL module to run and
setting up the Z-Spectroscopy configuration

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ForceDistanceCurve_OffsetComp.ZCtrl_SettlingTime
documentation: seconds to wait after switching off the Z-Ctrl

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_GenSweeper_MultipleSweeps
documentation: This example performs multiple generic sweeps with different limits and number of steps
In order to use this example, you will first need to set up the
generic sweep module (i.e. select the sweep signal and channels to acquire)

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_GenSweeper_MultipleSweeps.Sweep_Start
documentation: Sweep start values for the different sweeps to perfom

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_GenSweeper_MultipleSweeps.Sweep_Stop
documentation: (the list size determines the number of sweeps)

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_GenSweeper_MultipleSweeps.Sweep_InitSettling
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_GenSweeper_MultipleSweeps.Sweep_Settling
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_GenSweeper_MultipleSweeps.Sweep_Period
documentation: miliseconds

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_GenSweeper_MultipleSweeps.Sweep_MaxSlewRate
documentation: units/second

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_GenSweeper_MultipleSweeps.GenSweeper_NrAcqChannels
documentation: the swept channel is part of the acquired data

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ScansAtDifferentBias
documentation: This example performs multiple scans,
increasing the bias voltage by a defined increment after each scan.

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_ScansAtDifferentBias.Scan_Timeout
documentation: timeout in miliseconds to wait for the scan to finish

modules.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep_Triggered
documentation: This example performs a Frequency Sweep using the built-in functionality
in the RF Generator module

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep_Triggered.Limit_Lower
documentation: Hz

documentation: Hz

documentation: s

documentation: Off

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep_Triggered.OffTime
documentation: s

variables.name: nanonis_spm_examples.Example_RFGen_FreqSweep_Triggered.AutoOffTime
